title: HeadObject, GetObject, Conditions, Ranges, and Cleanup · Runtime Contract: Range Download
summary: A download service calls GET to discover metadata, leaks response bodies, retries after sending headers, and returns full private objects for malformed ranges. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the range download increment in bounded S3 download decision and cleanup flow. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Validate one bounded range, map inclusive offsets, cap response bytes, preserve Content-Range, and reject unsatisfiable or abusive requests.
Map application validators and preconditions to S3 requests and preserve 304, 412, not-found, and version-specific meaning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Range Download · Theory Range Download
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 8093 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a versioned conditional range and prove S3 response-body cleanup; cap bytes, time, attempts, concurrency, and spend before starting work; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Range Download contract for the supplied data.
